questions.question: Explain the difference between Bubble Sort and Selection Sort algorithms.
questions.hint: Both are sorting algorithms, but Bubble Sort involves multiple passes, whereas Selection Sort only passes through the list once.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of Insertion Sort for already sorted lists?
questions.hint: The time complexity of Insertion Sort for already sorted lists is O(n)

questions.question: Write a step-by-step algorithm for Bubble Sort on an unsorted list of 5 integers.
questions.hint: Start by comparing adjacent elements, swapping them if they are in the wrong order, and repeat this process until no more swaps are needed.
